text: Ka bonso, ka yɛ kyerɛ bonsuuri yɛlɛ? Yɛ kaa nyɛ a baa pɛmpɛllɛ naŋ maŋ baara lɛ. A ba tonɔ toma bee a wuoro pɛmɛ,
translated: Where are you going? We are going to the market to buy some things. We will go to the market early in the morning.

ref: You are a bunch of evil snakes, so how can you say anything good? Your words show what is in your hearts.
text: Yɛnee dompane boorɔ ŋa! Yɛ naŋ waa nenfaare, wola ka yɛ na toɔ̃ yeli yelsonne? Bonso, bonna naŋ be a sukyiri poɔ la ka a noɔre maŋ yele.
translated: You people are stubborn! We have been waiting for you, when will you come and tell us the truth?  If you don't want to tell us, then we will go and find out ourselves.

ref: People were eating, drinking, and getting married right up to the day the flood came and Noah went into the big boat.
text: Aŋa a bebie na poɔ a kɔmpiiri naŋ da ba wa sɛre, ka noba da dire kyɛ nyuuro, ka dɔbɔ derɛ pɔgeba, ka pɔgeba meŋ kulo serebɛ, a te ta daare na a Nowa naŋ kpɛ a gbori,
translated: If you want to eat, you should first wash your hands, then wash your face, then wash your body, then wash your clothes, then you should go and eat. You should not eat without washing.

ref: Everyone answered, “We and our own families will take the blame for his death!”
text: Lɛ la ka a noba zaa pabo laŋ yeli, “Ka a Yeezu zẽẽ do te ne te biiri.”
translated: And he said to them, “Have you not heard that Jesus said, ‘It is not what goes into the mouth that defiles a person, but what comes out of the mouth’?”

ref: You blind fools! Which is greater, the gold or the temple that makes the gold sacred?
text: Yɛnee zɔndambolo ŋa! Buo la kpoŋ, a salema bee a Naaŋmen die naŋ e ka a salema leɛ bonsoŋ?
translated: Is this the way to go? If you go, will you not come back? If you go, will you not come back?

ref: If one of my followers sins against you, go and point out what was wrong. But do it in private, just between the two of you. If that person listens, you have won back a follower.
text: “Ka a puoruu laŋne neɛ kanzaa wa e yeli kaŋ sãã fo poɔ, gaa ka fo ne o yoŋ te zeŋ ka fo wuli o fo posaanaa. Ka o naŋ wa sage saanaa, fo faa o la.
translated: If you are going to the river to fetch water, you should go early in the morning before the sun is too hot, so that you will not be tired when you return home. If you go when the sun is hot, you will be tired.

ref: So the words of the prophet Jeremiah came true, “They took the thirty silver coins, the price of a person among the people of Israel.
text: A ŋaa da eɛɛ la ka ka a Ŋmen-yelmanna Gyɛremaaya naŋ da yeli lɛ na tu a tuubu, “Ba da de la a warbie lezare ne pie na, naŋ da waa a soba na daaroŋ, a lɛ a Izerayɛl noba mine naŋ da sage ka a e O daaroŋ,
translated: And the people said to him, “We have heard that you are a prophet, and we have come to you from afar.
